conbody.p: Overview: Quality Alerts report enables the CDS Managers to accurately measure the CDS impact on PSI, PDI, NQI alerts and ensure timely action.

The report provides answers to the following questions:
How many alerts were generated for a specific PSI/PDI/NQI alert category?
How many alerts were resolved by adding a specific exclusion code?
How many quality clarifications were created? How many of those were agreed?
How many alerts were resolved as present on admission?
What are the details around alerts?
Navigation: On the Analytics home page left side panel, navigate to CDE One CDI Operations Quality Alerts .

Quality Alerts displays the following:
KPI Cards: Alerts, Resolved
Quality Alerts Summary Tabular Chart
Quality Alerts Detail Tabular Chart
On the top panel, you can select desired values for the slicers (refer ) and the corresponding data gets generated in the tabular chart.

Default Display: Default Display: By default, the dashboard displays the data for the inpatient encounters admitted in the last one calendar month. Click the ‘Reset’ button to reset both admit date and discharge date and select the required date.

row.entry:
The count of overall alerts generated for the selected period.
Example: 25

The count and percentage of the total alerts resolved.
Example: 5 (20%)

conbody.p: You can group the information in the Quality Alerts Summary visual with the help of the following criteria.

ul.li: Summarizes the quality alert information by CDS.

‘Value/Group By’ column displays the name of the CDS assigned to the encounters with quality alerts (PSI/PDI/NQI).
Example: Jones,Mary ; Earl, Tabitha
'Value/Group By' column displays the list of the CDS in alphabetical order sorted by their last name.
Default option selected when you open the report
Summarizes the quality alert information by all the three Alert Categories (PSI, PDI, NQI).
'Value/Group By' column displays the three Alert Categories in alphabetical order (NQI Topic, PDI Topic, PSI Topic).
Summarizes the quality alert information by PSI Topics.

‘Value/Group By’ column displays the ID and Description of the PSI Topics.
Example: PSI-13 Postoperative Sepsis

li.p: 'Value/Group By' column displays the list of the PSI Topics in ascending order.

ul.li: Summarizes the quality alert information by PDI Topics.

‘Value/Group By’ column displays the ID and Description of the PDI Topics.
Example: PDI-09 Postoperative Respiratory Failure Rate
'Value/Group By' column will display the list of the PDI Topics in ascending order.
Summarizes the quality alert information by NQI Topics.

‘Value/Group By’ column displays the ID and Description of the NQI Topics.
Example: NQI-3 Neonatal Blood Stream Infection
'Value/Group By' column displays the list of the NQI Topics in ascending order.
Provides a monthly summary of the quality alert information for the selected admit date range.

li.p: 'Value/Group By' column displays the month and year of the Admit Date selected in the below format:
YYYY-MM(Mon) ,
Example: 2020-05(May)

ul.li: Data is sorted and displayed by Admit Date in ascending order. Click the column header in Quality Alerts Summary -Group by/Value column to sort it in descending order.

li.p: Example: If you select the Admit Date filter from 23rd Dec, 2019 to 5th Apr, 2020 the 'Value/Group By' column displays the information as below:
2019-12(Dec) → (Quality alerts generated from 23rd Dec, 2019- 31st Dec, 2019 get displayed)
2020-04(Apr) → (Quality Alerts generated from 1st Apr, 2020- 5th Apr, 2020 should be displayed)
Provides a weekly summary of the quality alert information for the selected admit date range.
'Value/Group By' column displays the month and year selected in the below format:
YYYY-MM-DD - YYYY-MM-DD (Example : 2020-04-26 - 2020-05-02)

ul.li: The default start day of the week is considered to be Sunday.

li.p: Provides quarterly summary of the quality alert information for the selected admit date range.
'Value/Group By' column displays the month , year and Admit Date selected.
Format:YYYY-MM(Mon) - YYYY-MM(Mon), Example: 2020-05(Mar) - 2020-05(May)

ul.li: Provides a monthly summary of the quality alert information for the selected discharge date range.

li.p: 'Value/Group By' column displays the month and year of the Discharge Date selected.
Format: YYYY-MM(Mon) ,
Example: 2020-05(May)

ul.li: Data is sorted and displayed by Discharge Date in ascending order. Click the column header in Quality Alerts Summary -Group by/Value column to sort it in descending order.

li.p: Example: If the user selects the Discharge Date filter from 23rd Dec, 2019 to 5th Apr, 2020 the 'Value/Group By' column will display the information as below:
2019-12(Dec) → (Quality alerts generated from 23rd Dec, 2019- 31st Dec, 2019 should be displayed)
2020-04(Apr) → (Quality Alerts generated from 1st Apr, 2020- 5th Apr, 2020 should be displayed)

ul.li: Provides a weekly summary of the quality alert information for the selected discharge date range.

li.p: 'Value/Group By' column displays the month and year selected.
Format: YYYY-MM-DD - YYYY-MM-DD (Example : 2020-04-26 - 2020-05-02)

ul.li: The default start day of the week is considered to be Sunday.
Provides a quarterly summary of the quality alert information for the selected discharge date range.

li.p: 'Value/Group By' column displays the month , year and Discharge Date selected.
Format:YYYY-MM(Mon) - YYYY-MM(Mon), Example: 2020-05(Mar) - 2020-05(May)

p.b: Quality Alerts - Quality Alerts Summary Tabular Chart

ul.li: This chart displays summarized information on CDS activity on Quality Alerts.

li.p: Chart title updates dynamically based on the Group-By option selected.
Example: If "PSI Topic" Group By option is selected, the chart title displays as "Quality Alerts Summary by PSI Topic"

row.entry:
The "Group By" parameter based on which the details get displayed.
Example: If "Encounter CDS" group by option is selected , the Quality Alerts Summary displays the data for the individual CDS's.

The count of the total Quality Alerts generated corresponding to the Group By parameter selected.
Example: If "Encounter CDS" group by option is selected, the total Quality Alerts generated for the encounters assigned to the individual CDS's get displayed.

entry.p: The count of the total active Quality Alerts corresponding to the Group By parameter selected.
Example: If "Encounter CDS" group by option is selected, the total Active Quality Alerts for the individual CDS's get displayed.

row.entry: Out of the total quality alerts generated, the count of resolved alerts for which CDS selects "No Exclusion - NQI Present"/ "No Exclusion - PDI Present"/ "No Exclusion - PSI Present" option from the Global Exclusion pop up based on the alert category to resolve the alert.

entry.p: Out of the total quality alerts generated, the count of resolved alerts for which CDS selects "Present on Admission" option.

p.note:
POA option can be used to resolve the alert can be used in two ways:
Adding the POA directly in the Review grid.
Selecting "Present on Admission" option from the Global Exclusion pop up

row.entry: Out of the total alerts generated, the count of resolved alerts with for which CDS selects a ‘Global Exclusion’ (other than POA and No Exclusion(Present) ) criteria. Example: Non-Elective Admission, No OR Procedure, MDC 14 (Pregnancy) PDX
Out of the total alerts generated, the count of resolved alerts with ‘Specific Exclusion’ criteria set by the CDS.

entry.p: Out of the total alerts generated, the count of resolved alerts for which quality clarifications were sent by the CDS.

ul.li:
CDS can send the clarifications to resolve the alerts in two ways:
Selecting the "Create new clarification on Code" option from the Add to drop down option in the "Specific Exclusions" pop up.
Selecting the "New Clarification" link directly from the Quality Alerts panel.
Only one clarification can be sent for each quality alert.

row.entry: This does not include the count of alerts that are active.
Out of total quality clarifications sent, the count of quality clarifications responded by the providers.
Active Alerts are not be considered. Only clarifications with a Final response will be counted.

entry.p: The percentage of the total quality clarifications responded by the provider.
%Responded = ( Quality Clarifications Responded/Quality Clarifications Sent)*100

row.entry: Out of total quality clarifications responded, the count of quality clarifications agreed by the providers.

entry.p: The percentage of the total quality clarifications agreed by the provider.
%Agreed = ( Quality Clarifications Agreed/Quality Clarifications Responded)*100
Shows the total count for each of the columns in this table. Percentage is shown only for %Responded and %Agreed columns.
Example: Total count of alerts generated for a single individual CDS.

row.entry: %Responded column total = (Clarifications Responded column total)/(Clarifications Sent column total) *100% Agreed column total = (Clarifications Agreed column total)/(Clarifications Responded column total) *100

p.b: Quality Alerts - Quality Alerts Detail Tabular Chart

ul.li: This chart displays detailed information about individual quality alerts.
Multiple rows of single encounter are displayed if there are more than one quality alert raised.
Sort: Data is sorted based on Discharge Date in descending order.
Info icon(text on hover): Data in this chart displays detailed information for individual quality alerts.

row.entry: The Facility associated with the encounter.

entry.p: The Account number assigned to the encounter.

p.note: There will be multiple rows for the same account number if we have more than one quality alerts triggered and each row will specify details of one quality alert.

row.entry: The Admit Date and time assigned to the encounter.

The Discharge Date and time assigned to the encounter.
Default: Data in this report gets sorted by Discharge Date in descending order, most recent on top.

entry.p: The name of the CDS assigned to the encounter.
Format: Last Name, First Name (CDS ID)

row.entry:
The Type of Quality Alert generated.
Example: PSI, PDI, NQI

entry.p: The specific type of the Quality Alert generated.
Example: "PSI-13 Postop Sepsis " / "PDI-2 Pediatric Pressure Ulcer" / "NQI-3 Neonatal Blood Stream Infection"
The current status of the Quality Alert.
Example: Open/Resolved
The CM/PCS code along with the description for which Quality Alert was triggered.
Example: A41.9 Sepsis Unspecified

row.entry: The Global Exclusion selected by the CDS to resolve the Quality Alert.
The Specific Exclusion selected by the CDS to add to the Review screen to resolve the Quality Alert.

entry.p: The CM/PCS code for which the Clarification was raised by the CDS.

p.note:
The Clarification code can be selected via 2 options:
Selecting the "New Clarification" link directly from the Quality Alerts panel.
Selecting the "Create new clarification on Code" option from the Add to drop down option in the "Specific Exclusions" pop up.

row.entry:
The type of the clarification that was raised by the CDS.
Example: PSI/PDI/NQI based on the Quality Alert triggered
The column displays the Primary Reconciliation Type assigned to the clarification.Note: Only one Primary Reconciliation Type per Clarification is displayed.
The column displays the Other Reconciliation Types assigned to the clarification.Note: One or more Other Reconciliation Types per Clarification is available and are displayed by comma separated.
The Date and Time when the clarification was sent by the CDS.

entry.p: The Response received by the provider.
Example: Pending, Discuss, Agreed etc

row.entry: The Date and Time when the clarification was responded to by the provider.
Displays the name of the Provider that responded to the clarification.
The Encounter status associated with the encounter.
The name of the Payer assigned to the encounter.
The name of the Attending Provider assigned to the encounter.

The Visit Type of the encounter.
Example: I

ul.li: Displays the admit date range here. You can select required dates from the date lookup.
Format: Between Date range, Example: 7/1/2020 - 7/31/2020
Default: Last 1 calendar months date range selected by default.
Displays the discharge date range here. You can select required dates from the date lookup.

Format: Between Date range,
Example: 7/1/2020 - 7/31/2020
With the Reset button, you can reset the admit date range.
Tooltip: Use "Cltr + Click" to reset the Admit Date.
Displays a list of active and inactive facilities that you have access to. The list is sorted alphabetically.
You can select multiple facilities
You can search the facility by Facility ID or Description.
Format: Facility Description (Facility ID)
Default: ALL
Displays a list of active and inactive payers associated with the facilities that you have access to. The list is sorted alphabetically.
You can select multiple payers
"Unassigned Payer" displays data for the encounters that do not have a payer assigned.
You can search the Payer by Payer ID or Description.
Format: Payer Description (Payer ID)
Default: ALL
Displays a list of active and inactive CDSs associated with the facilities you have access to.
The CDS Filter in all the reports displays the users with at-least CDS or CDS Manager role assigned.
"Unassigned CDS" displays data for the encounters that do not have a CDS assigned.
Format: Last Name, First Name MI (CDS ID)
Default: ALL
Displays a list of topics of the different quality alert categories (NQI/PDI/PSI).

Format: Topic Topic Name ,
Example: PSI 13 Postop Sepsis

li.p: This filter displays the only list of Topics that are currently configured for the Organization.

ul.li: The complete description of the topics (NQI/PDI/PSI) gets displayed in the tooltip.

Displays the available patient types.
Example: Inpatient, Outpatient.
Default: Inpatient
Displays the list of available Visit Types.
You can select multiple Visit Types.
You can search the Visit Type.
Default: ALL
Displays the list of available units.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Unit.
Default: ALL
Displays Weekend Only Stay values (Y/N).
You can search the Weekend Only Stay.
Default: ALL
Displays the list of available Encounter Statuses.
You can select multiple Encounter Statuses.
You can search the Encounter Status.
Default: Review Not Needed unchecked by default.
Displays the list of available Admit Services.
You can select multiple Admit Services.
You can search the Admit Service.
Default: ALL

row.entry: Count of overall alerts generated. E.g. 100
Count and percentage of the total alerts resolved. E.g 5 (20%)
Count of the total Quality Alerts generated as per the ‘Group By’ parameter selected.E.g. 15
Count of total active Quality Alerts as per the “Group By’ parameter selected.E.g. 2

entry.p: Clarifications Sent

row.entry: Count of resolved Quality alerts for which clarifications were sent by the CDS.E.g. 10
% Responded = ( Count of quality clarifications Responded by Provider/Quality Clarifications Sent) X100E.g. 60%
%Agreed = ( Count of quality clarifications Agreed by Provider/ Count of quality clarifications Responded by Provider) X100E.g. 100%
